hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The input 'abc' (composed of a literal 'a', literal 'b', literal 'c' with unusual character codes or just concatenated characters without spaces) should transform to exactly the string: 'a b c'. This demonstrates that tabs, carriage returns, newlines, and repeated spaces are collapsed into single spaces, and the original non-whitespace characters are preserved in order.

If the node count is 0, the output string must be exactly 'empty NodeList'. If the count is 5 or more, the output must be a comma-separated list of at least 5 'h1' selectors followed by the string '( +1 more )', e.g., 'h1, h1, h1, h1, h1 ( +1 more )'.

The 'actual' string must follow the exact pattern: 'Element <selector> has attribute \"<name>\" with value \"<value>\"'. For the specific test case provided, this results in the string: "Element input has attribute \"type\" with value \"password\" ". Note the specific space after 'password' as shown in the exact value string.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same node description structure as used for 'all' kind selectors
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The 'node' object in the input should mirror the structure of the 'node' object in C004, containing keys like 'kind', 'value', and potentially a 'document' string representing the HTML root, though C004's output structure describes what the function returns rather than the input schema which is shared across description operations.

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to the active DOM element selection logic in the test suite examples
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The 'actual' value for focused_state operations corresponds to the exact selector string of the currently focused element in the test context, which varies by test case but follows the same selection logic as the 'focus' argument validation in C014.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature12_disabled_state.json.description: Assert disabled state of selected controls

feature13_focus_state.json.description: Assert current focus and not-focus state

feature14_unexpected_target_errors.json.description: Normalize errors for unsupported assertion targets

feature2_node_description.json.description: Describe DOM targets as compact selectors

feature3_presence.json.description: Assert that selected elements exist with optional counts

feature4_absence.json.description: Assert that selected elements do not exist

feature7_class_matching.json.description: Assert CSS class membership and absence

feature8_text_equality.json.description: Assert complete text content after whitespace normalization

_constraints.input_condition: All operations including normalized output or error cases
_constraints.output_rule: JSON object must be valid. Operations that succeed return an object with a 'value' or 'assertions' key. Operations that fail with an unsupported target return an object with an 'error', 'target_type', and 'target_value' key.

_constraints.input_condition: The 'operation' field in the request body
_constraints.output_rule: The system MUST handle the following specific operation identifiers. Other values trigger an error (implied by C001/Feature 14 or generic dispatch failure).
The 'input' object MUST contain a string field named 'text'.
The 'input' object MUST contain a string field named 'document' (HTML string) and an object field named 'node'.
The 'input' object MUST contain string 'document' and object 'target' (with 'kind' and 'value'). It MAY contain an object 'arguments' with optional integer 'count'.
The 'input' object MUST contain string 'document', object 'target', and object 'root' (type inferred from base cases but structurally identical to 'target').
The 'input' object must include 'arguments' with mandatory string 'name'. For 'attribute_matches', it must include 'expected' which can be a string or an object with 'kind' ('regex') and 'pattern'.
The 'input' object must include 'arguments' with mandatory string 'name'.
The 'input' object must include 'arguments' with mandatory string 'expected'.
The 'input' object must include 'arguments' with mandatory string 'expected'.
The 'input' object must include 'arguments' with mandatory object 'expected' containing 'kind' ('regex') and 'pattern'.
The 'input' object MUST include an array 'values' with objects containing 'selector' (string) and 'value' (string).
The 'input' object must include target object with 'kind' and 'value', ignoring 'arguments' as they are not present in test cases for this operation.
The 'input' object MUST include the string field 'focus' (target selector) and object 'target'.

_constraints.input_condition: Invalid 'kind' or primitive 'value' inside 'target' object for DOM operation (e.g., number, boolean)
_constraints.output_rule: Return an error object with 'error' key set to 'unexpected_target', 'target_type' set to the primitive type name, and 'target_value' set to the string representation of the primitive.
Set 'satisfied' to true if at least one element matches the selector unless 'arguments.count' is present. If 'count' exists, 'satisfied' is true only if the match count equals 'arguments.count'.
Set 'satisfied' to true if match count in root is 0. If match count > 0, 'satisfied' is false.
Treat tabs, carriage returns, newlines, and repeated spaces as ordinary whitespace, collapsed into one space. Trim beginning and end. Non-whitespace characters remain in original order.
Generate a list of up to 5 descriptions. If count > 5, append '( +1 more )'. If count is 0, output 'empty NodeList'.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation produces a valid assertion (presence, absence, attribute, class, text, value, disabled, focused)
_constraints.output_rule: The 'assertions' array must contain objects with string keys: 'satisfied' (bool), 'actual' (string), 'expected' (string), 'message' (string).
'actual' must be formatted as: 'Element <selector> has attribute \"<name>\" with value \"<value>\"'
exact_values: Element input has attribute \"type\" with value \"password\"

_constraints.output_rule: 'actual' must be formatted as: 'Element <selector> has attribute \"<name>\" with value \"<value>\"'
exact_values: Element input has attribute \"type\" with value \"password\"

_constraints.output_rule: 'actual' must be formatted as the concatenated class name string separated by spaces.
'actual' must be the normalized complete text of the element.
'actual' must be the normalized complete text of the element.
'actual' must be the string value of the form control.
'actual' must be 'Element <selector> is disabled' or 'Element <selector> is not disabled' (Case sensitivity on 'is' and capitalization of selector inferred from tests).
'actual' must be the selector where focus currently resides. The 'expected' field is the selector provided in the 'focus' argument.
Return the selector value unchanged in the 'value' field.
'expected' must be formatted as '/<pattern>/'.

This constraint describes that the 'actual' field for class operations must be the concatenated space-separated string of all matching CSS selector classes (ignoring the tag name unless specified in selector). The example output represents a DOM element currently matching the classes 'secret', 'password', 'input', and 'foo', formatted as: "secret-password-input foo".
